title: Market Brief — Saturday, May 16, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and MI300X at 60% signal oversupply on last-gen silicon, while B200 at 25% sits at the scarcity threshold and GB200 at 10% availability flags a severe shortage. The forward curve is screaming: B200 spot at $4.20/hr with a $3.50-$5.50 range shows buyers stretching for next-gen compute as legacy iron goes begging.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and MI300X at 60% signal oversupply on last-gen silicon, while B200 at 25% sits at the scarcity threshold and GB200 at 10% availability flags a severe shortage. The forward curve is screaming: B200 spot at $4.20/hr with a $3.50-$5.50 range shows buyers stretching for next-gen compute as legacy iron goes begging.

On Llama 70B reference, every GPU except B200 bleeds cash at medium-tier pricing, with MI300X the worst at $9.52/M raw ($11.90/M TCO-adjusted) versus $3/M medium revenue, a $8.90/M per-token loss. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling far below any TCO-adjusted production cost and is burning cash to buy market share. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when Claude Opus 4.6 delivers frontier reasoning at $25/M, a 69% discount for comparable capability. Undervalued trade: accumulate B200 spot capacity near $3.50/hr floor and sell inference at premium tier where margins hit 91%.
bullets: 85% availability on A100s confirms oversupply of legacy compute with no supply constraints.
10% availability on GB200 marks a severe shortage of next-gen inference hardware.
$11.90/M TCO-adjusted cost on MI300X makes AMD the most expensive inference path per token.
21.41 on the LLMflation index means token prices have fallen 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 undercuts the next cheapest model by 37%.
91% margin on B200 at premium tier makes it the only GPU generating strong returns across all pricing bands.
